cases.title: Testing-plan QR token behavior row: expired token rejected and logged
preconditions: Class session is Open with a QRSessionToken issued for that session.
Token expiresAt is more than 30 seconds after issuedAt per MVP default TTL.
Student is authenticated and actively enrolled in the session class section.
Student has no prior successful AttendanceRecord for this session.
steps: Advance server time beyond the token expiresAt value.
Submit check-in command through the application service boundary with the expired qrToken.
Query CheckInAttempt rows for the student-session-token tuple.
Query AttendanceRecord rows for the same student-session pair.
cases.expected: Check-in is rejected with outcome ExpiredQr; exactly one failed CheckInAttempt is persisted with token reference and timestamp; no Present or Late AttendanceRecord is created.

cases.title: Check-in service persists ExpiredQr attempt without attendance mutation
preconditions: Integration harness uses real DB transaction boundaries for qr_session_tokens, check_in_attempts, and attendance_records.
Open session row exists with an expired QRSessionToken whose classSessionId matches the target session.
Authenticated enrolled student has no success attendance row for the session.
steps: Invoke POST /v1/check-ins command path with the expired qrToken.
Assert the command returns terminal outcome ExpiredQr.
Query check_in_attempts for the submitted correlation/request id.
Query attendance_records for the student-session pair.
Commit and re-read the persisted rows outside the command transaction.
cases.expected: The DB contains one CheckInAttempt with outcome ExpiredQr and no successful AttendanceRecord for the same student-session; persisted data remains visible after transaction commit.

cases.title: One valid QR token can be used by multiple enrolled students within TTL
preconditions: Class session is Open with one QRSessionToken in Valid state and expiresAt in the future.
At least three authenticated Student actors are actively enrolled in the class section.
No enrolled test student has an existing AttendanceRecord for the session.
Effective policy does not require GPS or all supplied GPS data is valid.
steps: Submit check-in command for Student A with the shared valid qrToken.
Submit check-in command for Student B with the same qrToken before expiresAt.
Submit check-in command for Student C with the same qrToken before expiresAt.
Query CheckInAttempt and AttendanceRecord rows for all three students.
Verify the token remains bound to the original ClassSession and was not globally consumed by the first success.
cases.expected: All enrolled students are processed independently; each valid request can create its own Success attempt and Present/Late AttendanceRecord; no request is rejected solely because another student used the same token.

cases.title: POST /v1/check-ins with expired QR returns 422 ExpiredQr envelope
preconditions: Class session is Open.
QR token was issued for the open session and is now past its 30-second TTL by server time.
Enrolled Student is authenticated with Authorization bearer token.
Request includes Idempotency-Key and X-Request-Id headers.
steps: Send POST /v1/check-ins with qrToken and clientTimestamp in JSON body.
Validate HTTP status code and response envelope shape {data, meta, error}.
Assert data is null and error.code is ExpiredQr.
Assert error.message is localized Vietnamese copy instructing the student to scan the current QR on the lecturer screen.
Assert meta.requestId and meta.timestamp are present.
Query the attendance roster or DB and confirm no success attendance row was created.
cases.expected: HTTP 422 rule-failure response with standard envelope; error.code ExpiredQr; localized current-QR guidance is returned; failed attempt is logged and attendance is unchanged.

cases.title: QR TTL boundary uses server-side 30-second expiry
preconditions: Open session has a QR token with deterministic issuedAt timestamp.
Authenticated enrolled student has no prior success attendance.
Test harness can control or freeze server time around the token expiresAt boundary.
steps: At issuedAt plus 29 seconds, submit POST /v1/check-ins with the token and otherwise valid payload.
Reset fixture state for a second student or second session using a token with the same deterministic issuedAt.
At issuedAt plus 30 seconds plus a minimal epsilon, submit POST /v1/check-ins with that token.
Compare returned outcomes and persisted CheckInAttempt rows.
Verify clientTimestamp skew does not extend the token lifetime.
cases.expected: The before-expiry request can proceed to Success when all later rules pass; the after-expiry request is rejected with ExpiredQr; server time, not clientTimestamp, controls token expiry.

cases.title: Rule-order short-circuit: session state failures precede expired QR evaluation
preconditions: Student actor is authenticated.
One Scheduled session and one Closed session each have an expired or stale qrToken fixture.
The student is enrolled so enrollment would not mask the session-state result.
steps: Submit POST /v1/check-ins with expired token bound to the Scheduled session.
Assert the returned code is SessionNotOpen, not ExpiredQr.
Submit POST /v1/check-ins with expired token bound to the Closed session.
Assert the returned code is SessionClosed, not ExpiredQr.
Confirm failed attempts are logged with the first terminal outcome selected by validation order.
cases.expected: Validation follows documented order: authentication then session state before token integrity and expiry; BR-03 is not evaluated when BR-01 or BR-02 has already rejected the request.

cases.title: Rule-order short-circuit: ExpiredQr precedes enrollment, duplicate, and GPS checks
preconditions: Class session is Open.
Expired qrToken is bound to the open session.
Fixtures include at least one unenrolled student, one student with prior success, and one GPS-required session variant with missing or invalid GPS.
All actors are authenticated Student users.
steps: Submit POST /v1/check-ins as an unenrolled student with the expired token.
Assert error.code is ExpiredQr, not NotEnrolled.
Submit as a student with a prior successful AttendanceRecord using the expired token.
Assert error.code is ExpiredQr, not DuplicateCheckIn.
Submit with missing or invalid GPS using the expired token where GPS is required.
Assert error.code is ExpiredQr, not GpsRequired, GpsDisabled, OutOfRadius, or LowAccuracy.
cases.expected: For an Open session, token expiry is the first failing rule after session state; later BR-06, BR-07, and BR-08 to BR-10 checks are not evaluated when the QR token is expired.

cases.title: Duplicate replay with valid BR-03 token preserves one success per student-session
preconditions: Open session has a Valid multi-use QR token within TTL.
Authenticated enrolled student has no prior success attendance.
Harness can replay POST /v1/check-ins with identical and distinct Idempotency-Key headers.
steps: Submit POST /v1/check-ins with the valid token and Idempotency-Key A.
Replay the exact same request with Idempotency-Key A.
Submit another check-in for the same student-session-token with new Idempotency-Key B before token expiry.
Query attendance_records and check_in_attempts for the student-session pair.
Verify the QR token was not made globally one-time-use by the first success.
cases.expected: Replay with the same idempotency key returns the prior success result without adding a second success row; the new-key duplicate is rejected with DuplicateCheckIn; there is still exactly one successful AttendanceRecord.

cases.title: Expired QR retry storm returns deterministic ExpiredQr without success writes
preconditions: Open session has an expired QR token.
Authenticated enrolled student has no AttendanceRecord for the session.
Harness can send concurrent POST /v1/check-ins requests with the same actor, token, and idempotency key.
steps: Launch multiple parallel check-in requests using the expired token and same Idempotency-Key.
Collect all command outcomes and response envelopes.
Query check_in_attempts for the correlation ids or idempotency record.
Query attendance_records for the student-session pair.
cases.expected: Every replay observes deterministic ExpiredQr behavior; idempotency returns the prior failure or equivalent failure envelope; no Present or Late AttendanceRecord is inserted under retry pressure.

cases.title: Student and cross-scope Lecturer cannot fetch current QR metadata
preconditions: Open session exists in section A assigned to Lecturer A with a current Valid QR token.
Student user is authenticated.
Lecturer B is authenticated but is not assigned to section A.
AcademicAdmin or Lecturer A positive-control actor is available.
steps: Student calls GET /v1/class-sessions/{sessionId}/qr/current for section A.
Assert HTTP 403 Forbidden or OutOfScope and no qrPayload or expiresAt leaks in response data.
Lecturer B calls GET /v1/class-sessions/{sessionId}/qr/current for section A.
Assert HTTP 403 OutOfScope or Forbidden and no QR payload leakage.
Lecturer A or AcademicAdmin calls the same endpoint and receives tokenState Valid with expiresAt.
cases.expected: Unauthorized actors cannot obtain current QR token metadata; scoped actor can fetch the current QR; no cross-scope or role-denied response leaks qrPayload or token timing.

cases.title: Student export and cross-scope Lecturer report deny access to sessions with ExpiredQr attempts
preconditions: Section A contains an Open session with at least one logged ExpiredQr CheckInAttempt.
Student user is authenticated.
Lecturer B is authenticated but not assigned to section A.
BR-18 scopes exports by actor role and BR-19 denies unauthorized report access.
steps: Student calls POST /v1/exports/attendance with filters targeting section A.
Assert HTTP 403 Forbidden and no exportJobId or partial data is returned.
Lecturer B calls GET /v1/reports/attendance with classSectionId for section A.
Assert HTTP 403 OutOfScope or Forbidden.
Verify denied responses do not reveal counts of ExpiredQr attempts or attendance rows in data or pagination metadata.
cases.expected: Student export is denied per BR-18; cross-scope Lecturer report access is denied per BR-19; restricted sessions and BR-03 failure data are not leaked through data payloads or pagination counts.

cases.title: Student UI shows expired QR guidance and allows rescan of current lecturer QR
preconditions: Lecturer has opened a session and the lecturer screen displays a rotating QR code with visible validity timing or refresh behavior.
Student is logged in on a supported mobile browser profile.
Student has scanned or opened a check-in deep link containing a QR token that has passed its 30-second TTL.
steps: Student submits the check-in form using the expired QR token.
Observe the error banner, inline alert, or modal shown on the student check-in page.
Verify Vietnamese copy instructs the student to scan the current QR on the lecturer screen.
On the lecturer view, wait for QR refresh and confirm a new/current QR is displayed without page reload.
Student scans the current QR and submits again while still within TTL and otherwise eligible.
Verify the success or next-step UI is shown only for the current QR submission.
cases.expected: The browser journey surfaces ExpiredQr as localized current-QR guidance, lecturer QR rotation remains visible, and a fresh within-TTL QR can proceed without treating the old expired token as valid.
